summary: Alabama's load-bearing citations (Ala. Code §§ 16-13B-1, 16-13B-2, 16-13B-7) were independently re-opened on the official Alabama Legislature ALISON portal via Firecrawl and the verbatim text confirms each substantive claim — the $40,000 competitive-sealed-bidding threshold for city/county boards of education, the (a)(9) sanitation/solid-waste-only renewal carve-out (no pupil-transportation exception), and the 5-year cap for personal-property/contractual-services contracts and 10-year cap for lease-purchase contracts. Amendment histories shown on the official source (16-13B-1: Acts 2009-760, 2023-203; 16-13B-2: Acts 2009-760, 2012-437, 2012-557, 2016-298, 2017-421, 2021-485, 2022-80; 16-13B-7: Acts 2009-760, 2016-298) align with the deliverables. No 2025 or 2026 enactment touching Chapter 13B was located on a fresh disconfirming search of the 2026 session (Alabama Daily News recap, ACLU 2026 summary, LegiScan AL 2026 index). The four substantive Alabama fields hold; District Variance properly held as Unknown. Two non-load-bearing P3 hygiene issues: (a) the JSON pack's Statutory Citation field omits the official URL that appears in the findings table cell, and (b) the JSON's Renewal Mechanics summary omits the 'within the 5-year cap, original competitively-bid contracts may contain price-adjustment, option-year, or multi-year terms' detail captured in the findings cell — not a contradiction, but a JSON-versus-prose terseness asymmetry.

citationVerifications.citation: Ala. Code § 16-13B-1 (2025) — Applicability; Local Preference Zone; Joint Agreement; Bid Bond
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Live ALISON section text confirms verbatim: '$40,000' threshold, 'made by or on behalf of any city or county board of education,' 'free and open competitive bidding, on sealed bids, to the lowest responsible bidder,' and Oct. 1, 2027 CPI-adjustment trigger in (a)(2). Amendment history (Act 2009-760; Act 2023-203) matches the deliverable. Public-works carve-out to Title 39 also confirmed.

citationVerifications.citation: Ala. Code § 16-13B-2 (2025) — Exceptions to Competitive Bidding Requirements
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Live ALISON section text confirms (a)(9) verbatim: 'Existing contracts up for renewal for sanitation or solid waste collection, recycling, or disposal and those providing the service.' (a)(13) cooperative purchasing through National Association of Counties / successor / other national or regional cooperatives subject to EOPA approval confirmed. (a)(15) GSA-contract carve-out confirmed. The full (a)(1)-(15) list contains NO pupil-transportation, school-bus, IEP, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or special-education transportation exception. Amendment history (Acts 2009-760, 2012-437, 2012-557, 2016-298, 2017-421, 2021-485, 2022-80) matches the deliverable.

citationVerifications.citation: Ala. Code § 16-13B-7 (2025) — Defaulting Bidder; Sole Source; Lease-Purchase Contracts
citationVerifications.claimedTierOrUrl: Tier 1 (codified state statute); https://alison.legislature.state.al.us/code-of-alabama?section=16-13B-7 (PRIMARY citation for Field 1 Max Term)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Live ALISON section text confirms (f) verbatim: 'Contracts for the purchase of personal property or contractual services shall be let for periods not greater than five years. Lease-purchase contracts for capital improvements and repairs to real property shall be let for periods not greater than 10 years and all other lease-purchase contracts shall be let for periods not greater than 10 years.' (a) second-lowest-responsible-bidder substitution 'for the remainder of the award period without rebidding' confirmed. (b) sole-source documentation requirements confirmed. Amendment history (Act 2009-760; Act 2016-298) matches the deliverable.

citationVerifications.citation: Ala. Code § 16-13B-2.1 (2025) — Child Nutrition Program Emergency Exception
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: ALISON sister-section URLs on the same portal (16-13B-1, -2, -7) re-opened and rendered verbatim; this section was not independently re-rendered during the bounded audit pass because Firecrawl became unavailable mid-session. Section appears in ALISON's Chapter 13B section list (verified during -7 fetch). Deliverable's quoted scope (60-day Child Nutrition Program emergency; State Superintendent recommendation; EOPA approval) is internally consistent with the section's title rendered on the portal and is non-load-bearing for the Max-Term/Rebid/Renewal fields.

Section appears in ALISON's Chapter 13B section list (rendered during -7 fetch). Independent section-text re-render was not completed (Firecrawl became unavailable). Quoted text in source-ledger ('In case of emergency affecting public health, safety, or convenience...contracts may be let to the extent necessary to meet the emergency without public advertisement') is corroborated by the ADECA 2021 / EPA 2025 manual structure and is non-load-bearing.

citationVerifications.citation: Ala. Code § 41-16-50 (2025) — General state/local bid law; $30,000 threshold
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Section not independently re-rendered in this audit pass (Firecrawl unavailable for follow-on fetches). However, the deliverable's claim that § 41-16-50 does NOT reach city/county boards of education is independently corroborated by current Justia 2025 Code recital and the deliverable's own statutory-history note (Act 2009-760 removed city/county BOEs from Title 41 to Title 16 Ch. 13B). Used only as a cross-reference / applicability-boundary citation; not load-bearing for any substantive Max-Term/Rebid/Renewal answer.

citationVerifications.citation: Ala. Code § 41-16-57 (2025) — Municipal/County 3-year cap on services
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Section not independently re-rendered (Firecrawl unavailable). However, a fresh WebSearch confirmed the verbatim quote 'Contracts for the purchase of personal property or contractual services shall be let for periods not greater than three years' from the 2025 Code of Alabama Title 41 ch. 16 art. 3 § 41-16-57, with the deliverable's interpretive boundary (3-year cap binds municipalities/counties/instrumentalities, NOT city/county BOEs after Act 2009-760) properly stated. Cross-reference only; not load-bearing.

citationVerifications.citation: Ala. Code Title 16 Ch. 27 (Transportation of Pupils) — TOC review
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Chapter heading 'Chapter 27 Transportation of Pupils. (§16-27-1 to §16-27-9)' confirmed in the ALISON Title-16 TOC sidebar rendered during the § 16-13B-7 fetch. The deliverable's interpretive claim that Ch. 27 contains no contract-term, rebid, or renewal provision for private contractors (and that § 16-27-6 references contracts only to require a seat-belt clause) was not independently re-verified section-by-section in this audit pass. Non-load-bearing for Max-Term / Rebid / Renewal.

citationVerifications.citation: Ala. Code Title 16 Ch. 27A (Alabama School Bus Safety Act) — TOC review
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Chapter heading 'Chapter 27A Alabama School Bus Safety Act. (§16-27A-1 to §16-27A-12)' confirmed in the ALISON Title-16 TOC sidebar. Substance (automated stop-arm camera enforcement) is non-load-bearing for the five fields.

citationVerifications.citation: Ala. Admin. Code ch. 290-2-4 — Transportation regulations
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: URL resolves (HTTP 200) to a PDF binary; WebFetch could not extract text and Firecrawl was unavailable in this pass to PDF-parse it. The chapter's existence and authority basis (Ala. Code §§ 16-3-11, 16-4-4, 16-27-1, 16-27-3 through -5, 32-5-8, 41-16-50 et seq.) are corroborated by the State Board's 2021 republished rules PDF located via fresh search. Deliverable's interpretive claim (no contract-term, rebid, or renewal provision for private contractors; basic safety / inspection / driver / mechanic / accident-report content only) was the original Stage-1/Stage-2 reading and is non-load-bearing for Max-Term/Rebid/Renewal.

citationVerifications.citation: Department of Examiners of Public Accounts, County/Local Competitive Bid and Public Works Law Manual (2025)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: URL resolves (HTTP 200, 1.2 MB PDF) with PDF metadata D:20250211141410Z confirming 2025 edition. WebFetch cannot extract text from the binary; Firecrawl PDF-parse not available in this pass. The deliverable's verbatim quote ('Contracts for the purchase of personal property or contractual services – not greater than five years. § 16-13B-7(f).') tracks § 16-13B-7(f) text re-verified directly on the codified statute, so use as a corroborator stands.

URL resolves (HTTP 200, PDF). Deliverable correctly flags the document predates Act 2023-203 (threshold change from $15,000 to $40,000); used as a corroborator on Field 1 (5-year cap), not the $40,000 threshold.

citationVerifications.citation: Dagley, Education Law for the Alabama Superintendent (2024 revision)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: URL resolves (HTTP 200, 1.6 MB, 253-page PDF). Used as corroborator only; the load-bearing finding rests on the statute itself.
URL resolves (HTTP 200, PDF). Predates Act 2023-203 but the 5/10-year cap statements remain accurate; corroborator only.
URL resolves (HTTP 200, 1.1 MB PDF). Used as currentness/session-law check; deliverable's claim that no 2025 enactment touches Chapter 13B is independently corroborated by fresh disconfirming search.

citationVerifications.citation: Alabama Daily News, '2026 Education Bills Recap'
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: URL resolves (HTTP 200, HTML). Live fetch confirms the 2026 enacted-education-bill list — none touch Chapter 13B / competitive bidding / school transportation procurement (HB78 screen time; SB99 Ten Commandments; SB149 military veteran teachers; HB565 CHEER Act, etc.).

mismatches: scratch/alabama.json tableRow.statutoryCitation omits the official URL ('Official URL: https://alison.legislature.state.al.us/code-of-alabama?section=16-13B-7') that is appended to the corresponding cell in research-findings.md. Not a contradiction; a JSON-versus-prose terseness gap (P3 hygiene).

scratch/alabama.json tableRow.renewalMechanics is a 2-sentence summary; research-findings.md adds a third sentence ('Within the 5-year cap, original competitively-bid contracts may contain price-adjustment, option-year, or multi-year terms provided the total term does not exceed 5 years and the option terms were in the original bid specifications'). Same legal position, different specificity (P3 hygiene).

verification-notes.md text 'For each load-bearing primary citation behind the Max-Term, Rebid, and Renewal answers, I re-opened the official Justia 2025 Code text via firecrawl scrape' is method documentation describing the original research run; the Post-Synthesis Citation-Grounding Correction Pass (also recorded in verification-notes.md) then re-opens the official ALISON sources. The two notes are consistent only if read in sequence; a reader skimming the first paragraph could conclude the load-bearing verification was on Justia rather than the official ALISON portal. P3 hygiene.

issues.evidence: Five PDF citations (EPA 2025 Manual, ALSDE Bid Laws Summary, Dagley 2024 Superintendent Manual, AASBO 2019, ASBA 2025 Enactments) reach 200 OK and are demonstrably present at the cited URLs. WebFetch cannot extract text from PDF binaries (returns 'application/pdf' raw stream); during this audit pass Firecrawl PDF-parsing was unavailable due to the MCP server reporting 'requires re-authorization (token expired).' The deliverable's verbatim quotes from these PDFs were not independently re-extracted in this audit pass; they remain corroborators only (load-bearing claims rest on the statutes themselves, which were verified verbatim).
issues.recommendedRemediation: On a later audit pass when Firecrawl is available, run firecrawl_scrape with parsers=['pdf'] against each PDF URL to independently re-confirm the verbatim quotes attributed to them. No deliverable text needs to change; this is a verification-completeness gap, not a defect in the deliverables.

issues.evidence: These section-text URLs were not independently re-rendered in this audit pass after Firecrawl became unavailable. The load-bearing § 16-13B-1, § 16-13B-2, and § 16-13B-7 URLs WERE re-rendered verbatim on the official ALISON portal. § 41-16-57's verbatim '3-year' cap text was confirmed via a fresh WebSearch quotation pulled from the 2025 Code of Alabama recital. Chapter TOC entries (Ch. 27 and 27A) were visible in the ALISON Title-16 TOC sidebar rendered during the § 16-13B-7 fetch.
issues.recommendedRemediation: On a future audit pass, re-open each remaining ALISON section URL via Firecrawl to round out the citation-by-citation verification. The verdicts on these sections are 'partially verified' (reachable in TOC, content not re-rendered in this pass), not 'unreachable'.

issues.evidence: scratch/alabama.json tableRow.statutoryCitation reads 'Ala. Code § 16-13B-7(f) (2025); Ala. Code § 16-13B-1(a)(1) (2025); Ala. Code § 16-13B-2 (2025).' The research-findings.md table cell extends this with 'Official URL: https://alison.legislature.state.al.us/code-of-alabama?section=16-13B-7' and parenthetical descriptors of each section's role.
issues.recommendedRemediation: If a future revision normalizes the JSON pack to mirror the findings cell, append the official URL and section-role descriptors. Strictly P3 hygiene — the two are not contradictory.

issues.evidence: scratch/alabama.json renewalMechanics is the shorter form: 'N/A as a statutory renewal-without-rebid mechanism for student-transportation contracts. The only renewal-without-rebid exception (§ 16-13B-2(a)(9)) is limited to sanitation/solid waste.' research-findings.md adds the 'Within the 5-year cap, original competitively-bid contracts may contain price-adjustment, option-year, or multi-year terms' sentence.
issues.recommendedRemediation: If JSON-prose parity is a goal, extend the JSON cell to mirror the findings cell. No legal contradiction (P3 hygiene).

issues.field: verification-notes.md (Alabama paragraph 1 vs. Post-Synthesis Correction Pass note)
issues.evidence: verification-notes.md ## Alabama opens with: 'For each load-bearing primary citation behind the Max-Term, Rebid, and Renewal answers, I re-opened the official Justia 2025 Code text via firecrawl scrape and confirmed verbatim that each section says what the pack claims.' Later in the same file, the 'Post-Synthesis Citation-Grounding Correction Pass (2026-06-23)' records that the correction pass re-pointed every citation URL from Justia/FindLaw/Public.Law to official ALISON sources and re-opened the official source. A reader skimming only the Alabama paragraph could mistake the final verification as Justia-based.
issues.recommendedRemediation: On a future revision, normalize the Alabama opening paragraph to refer to the ALISON re-verification (or note both stages) so the reader does not have to read the cross-state correction-pass note to reconcile.

issues.evidence: All five Alabama fields are populated; District Variance is properly marked Unknown with a documented negative search path in the Exceptions section. Challenge-matrix has the required 5 minimum rows (Max Term, Rebid, Renewal, Special-Ed, District Variance) plus 2 bonus rows (school-specific vs. general procurement applicability boundary; currentness). Coverage is complete.
issues.recommendedRemediation: No remediation needed; recording for completeness.
